Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Paris 2024 Olympics: Plan your SUNDAY viewing with Team SA to dazzle in the 100m event

Three South African men in the 100m semi-finals.

The Paris 2024 Olympics keep on delivering. Akani Simbine is one of three Team SA members to contest tonight’s 100m semi-finals.

South African sprint sensation Akani Simbine ran a time of 9.93 seconds at Tokyo 2020 in the 100m final. That meant he missed a bronze medal by a heartbreaking four-hundredths of a second behind Canada’s Andre De Grasse. Italy’s Lamont Marcell Jacobs was the surprise gold medalist four years ago in a wide-open field that Simbine fancied his chances of winning in the Land of the Rising Sun. At Rio 2016; medalists in the 100m event were iconic Jamaican Usain Bolt with gold, USA’s Justin Gatlin claiming silver and De Grasse scooping the bronze. Rio and Tokyo contain painful memories for Akani Simbine, but Paris 2024 can change all of that.

Shaun Maswanganyi and Benjamin Richardson join Simbine in the semi-finals contested at 20:05 on Sunday night. The final is at 21:50.Maswanganyi’s semi-final goes at 20:05, Simbine’s is at 20:14 and Richardson runs his semi at 20:23. 21-year-old Benjamin Richardson and United States-based Shaun Maswanganyi will run with Simbine in the 4x100m event but the fourth member of what should be a formidable quartet is still unclear. Jamaican star Oblique Seville, who has a nod of approval from the iconic Usain Bolt, set the world’s fastest time of 2024, clocking 9.82 seconds in June. He ran 9.99 in the heats.

Let us know by clicking on the comment tab below this article or emailinginfo@thesouthafrican.com. Meanwhile, you can also send a WhatsApp to 060 011 021 1. Follow@TheSAnews on XandThe South African on Facebookfor your social fix.
